Bus Timings

Big Bus Abu Dhabi Timetable

October 8, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Residents as well as visitors to Abu Dhabi have many convenient and efficient travel options among the public transportation options available. The most popular way to see the Abu Dhabi landmarks is with the Big Bus Tours Abu Dhabi, with daily tours.

In this article, we will look at the routes, operational details, ticket pricing and the major stops along the Big Bus Abu Dhabi routes.

Contents

Operational Details

Big Bus Tours Abu Dhabi provides punctual and reliable service every day. The city can be easily done on the City Tour (Red Route), which has buses leaving at regular intervals of every 30-40 minutes. Below is the schedule for the City Tour:

City Tour (Red Route) Timetable 1

City Tour (Red Route) Timetable 2

City Tour (Red Route) Timetable 3

City Tour (Red Route) Timetable 4- First Bus: 9:00am from Abu Dhabi Mall (Stop #1)

  • Last Full-Circuit Tour: 5:00pm from Abu Dhabi Mall
  • Full Circuit Time: Approximately 2 hours
  • Bus Frequency: Every 30-40 minutes

For the Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque Shuttle (Green Route), the buses run at specific times during the day, with a circuit time of 2.5 hours:

Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque Shuttle (Green Route) Timetable- First Bus: 12:00pm from Abu Dhabi Mall

  • Last Bus: 2:00pm from Abu Dhabi Mall
  • Full Circuit Time: Approximately 2.5 hours
  • Bus Frequency: Every 2.5 hours

Ticket Pricing: Big Bus Tours fares begin at AED 100 for a full day pass with unlimited hop-on, hop-off to the City Tour (Blue) route. You can buy your ticket online or on the bus.

Take Bus 401 and discover more about Abu Dhabi’s rich heritage.

Route Overview

1. City Tour Red Route

City Tour (Red Route)Abu Dhabi’s Red Route shows off the key attractions in the city. Below is an overview of the major stops along the City Tour:

  1. Abu Dhabi Mall: The starting point for the tour, located at Al Maiyani Street / 10th Street near Tim Hortons, and a major shopping destination.

  2. Louvre Museum: A world-renowned museum housing art from various cultures, located at Jacques Chirac Street (Louvre Parking Area Bus Stop) on Saadiyat Island. Nearby attractions include Manarat Al Saadiyat and the UAE Pavilion.

  3. Abu Dhabi Promenade: A beautiful waterfront area located on Corniche Street, next to Al Kareem Mosque.

  4. Public Beach (Corniche): A popular spot for beach lovers and relaxation, situated on Corniche Street near Corniche Beach.

  5. Al Bahar Beach: Another scenic beach located on Corniche Street at the Al Bahar Beach entrance.

  6. Heritage Village: A fascinating museum showcasing traditional Emirati life, located on Al Alam Street near its main entrance.

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Marina Mall: A large mall offering shopping and dining options, located on Al Marsa Street near the Marina Eye.

  2. Bay View: A serene spot on Al Kasir Street, located next to Al Kareem Mosque.

  3. Founder’s Memorial: A tribute to the UAE’s founding father, located on Al Marsa Street at the Founder’s Memorial drop-off area.

10.

  1. Presidential Palace: The official residence of the UAE’s leadership, located on West Corniche Road near the Visitors’ Centre.

  2. Al Hosn Fort: A historical fort and symbol of the city, located on Hamdan Street. Nearby attractions include Al Manhal Palace & Gardens and The Landmark.

  3. WTC Souk: A traditional marketplace offering local goods, located on Khalifa bin Zayed The First Street near the World Trade Center’s main entrance.

14.

2. jpg)

The route includes:

  1. Abu Dhabi Mall: The pick-up point for passengers.

  2. Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque: One of the largest mosques in the world, showcasing stunning Islamic architecture and culture.

Nearby Attractions and Landmarks

Some of the key attractions along the Big Bus Tours Abu Dhabi routes include:

1.

2.

  1. Al Hosn Fort: A historical landmark that offers a glimpse into the city’s past.

4.

  1. Marina Mall & Sky Tower: A modern shopping mall with views of the city skyline.

  2. Heritage Village: A cultural museum that presents traditional life in the UAE.

  3. Louvre Museum: An art lover’s paradise located on Saadiyat Island.

  4. Public Beach (Corniche): A relaxing spot for a day by the sea.

Journey Experience

Hop-on, hop-off lets you spend as much time exploring the city’s incredible array of attractions, such as the cultural Heritage Village and the world’s famous Louvre Museum.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: October 8, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej

Al Khaleej is the lead editor at Dubai Time — covering UAE transport, banking, visa services, and city guides. Trusted by residents and expats across the Emirates for accurate, up-to-date local information.
